library_preparation_preflight.summary: org.osgi.service.log:1.4.0 is an API-only OSGi bundle whose public signatures reference OSGi Framework types and the new LogStreamProvider API references PushStream; the artifact POM does not declare these compile-time companions.

deterministic_setup.reason: The 1.4.0 bundle imports org.osgi.framework [1.8,2) and public APIs such as LogService, LoggerFactory, and LogEntry use Bundle and ServiceReference.

The 1.4.0 bundle adds org.osgi.service.log.stream.LogStreamProvider, whose createStream method returns org.osgi.util.pushstream.PushStream; the service.log POM does not bring this dependency transitively.

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property appears necessary. Treat the library as OSGi API/spec interfaces rather than a runnable log implementation: meaningful tests can use small in-memory stubs or dynamic proxies instead of starting an OSGi container. Ensure any stubs implementing LogService also implement the LoggerFactory methods added in 1.4.0, and any LogEntry stub implements the new 1.4 getters such as getLogLevel(), getLoggerName(), getSequence(), getThreadInfo(), and getLocation().
risks: Using the old 1.3.0 test stubs unchanged will keep failing javac because 1.4.0 adds abstract methods to LoggerFactory and LogEntry.

The library has no concrete logging implementation, so tests that try to obtain a real LogService from an OSGi runtime would need additional framework setup; prefer self-contained stubs unless local Gradle verification shows otherwise.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8356 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.osgi:org.osgi.service.log:1.4.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
